callback_profiler.h(3) Library Functions Manual callback_profiler.h(3)

NAME

callback_profiler.h - ⏱️ Profiling instrumentation for libwebsockets callbacks

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct lws_callback_stats_t
Callback profiling statistics for a single LWS callback reason.

Functions


bool lws_profiler_init (void)
Initialize the callback profiler. void lws_profiler_destroy (void)
Cleanup the callback profiler. uint64_t lws_profiler_start (int callback_reason, uint32_t client_id)
Record a callback invocation with timing. void lws_profiler_stop (uint64_t handle, size_t bytes_processed)
Stop timing a callback invocation. bool lws_profiler_get_stats (int callback_reason, lws_callback_stats_t *stats)
Get statistics for a specific callback. void lws_profiler_reset (void)
Reset all profiling statistics. void lws_profiler_dump_report (void)
Print profiling report to logs. char * lws_profiler_export_json (void)
Export statistics as JSON. bool lws_profiler_is_initialized (void)
Check if profiler is initialized.

Detailed Description

⏱️ Profiling instrumentation for libwebsockets callbacks

Tracks callback frequency, execution time, and data throughput for performance analysis and optimization.

Features:

  • Per-callback statistics (execution time, frequency, data processed)
  • Thread-safe counters with minimal overhead
  • Min/max/average timing aggregation
  • JSON reporting for analysis

Author

Claude (asciichat/polecats/furiosa)

Date

February 2026

Definition in file callback_profiler.h.

Function Documentation

void lws_profiler_destroy (void )

Cleanup the callback profiler. Frees all profiling structures and destroys mutex. Called at application exit or when stopping profiling.

void lws_profiler_dump_report (void )

Print profiling report to logs. Generates a human-readable summary of callback statistics including:

  • Invocation frequency per callback type
  • Average/min/max execution times
  • Total data processed
  • Efficiency metrics

char * lws_profiler_export_json (void )

Export statistics as JSON. Returns a JSON string with complete profiling data. Caller must free the returned string.

Returns

Allocated JSON string, or NULL on failure

bool lws_profiler_get_stats (int callback_reason, lws_callback_stats_t * stats)

Get statistics for a specific callback. Retrieves accumulated statistics for analysis and logging.

Parameters

callback_reason LWS callback reason enum (as integer)
stats Output structure (caller-allocated)

Returns

true if statistics exist, false if callback never invoked

References stats.

bool lws_profiler_init (void )

Initialize the callback profiler. Must be called once at application startup before any callbacks are profiled. Thread-safe operation is ensured via internal mutex.

Returns

true on success, false on failure

bool lws_profiler_is_initialized (void )

Check if profiler is initialized.

Returns

true if profiler is active, false otherwise

void lws_profiler_reset (void )

Reset all profiling statistics. Clears counters and timing data. Useful for collecting data over specific time windows.

uint64_t lws_profiler_start (int callback_reason, uint32_t client_id)

Record a callback invocation with timing. Call this when entering a callback to start timing. Returns an opaque handle used to stop timing.

Parameters

callback_reason LWS callback reason enum (as integer)
client_id Optional client identifier for per-client analysis

Returns

Opaque timing handle (non-zero if profiler is initialized)

void lws_profiler_stop (uint64_t handle, size_t bytes_processed)

Stop timing a callback invocation. Call this when exiting a callback to record execution time. Updates min/max/average statistics and invocation count.

Parameters

handle Timing handle returned by lws_profiler_start()
bytes_processed Bytes processed by this callback (0 if not applicable)

Author

Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>
